title: No one-step block-kind rename tool
description: Block-kind renames require a manual multi-op migration: create a new schema, register a new block_kind, register installed_schemas/installed_blocks entries, write migrated block data, replace relation_type endpoint kinds, optionally rename relation_type ids, remove old config entries, and validate. rename-canonical-id explicitly does not support block_kind, and no harness op performs the filesystem/config/schema/data/relation cascade as one validated transaction.

evidence.reference: rename-canonical-id supports kind item | relation_type | lens | layer, not block_kind
story block_kind rename to user-stories requires coordinated changes across block_kinds, installed_schemas, installed_blocks, schemas, block data, and relation_type endpoint kinds

impact: Renaming a block kind is high-friction and error-prone inside the pi harness: operators must sequence multiple config/schema/block writes by hand, old physical files can remain orphaned, and partial failure can leave config, relations, schemas, and data out of sync.
proposed_resolution: Add a first-class block-kind rename operation that performs the full cascade transactionally: create/rename schema and data surfaces, update block_kinds and installed registries, migrate block data, update relation_type source_kinds/target_kinds and hierarchy/lens references, optionally rename relation_type ids by policy, validate the resulting substrate, and report or clean orphaned old files.
